devanagari: अथ चतुर्थोऽध्यायः । श्रीराजोवाच । देवासुरनृणां सर्गो नागानां मृगपक्षिणाम् । सामासिकस्त्वया प्रोक्तो यस्तु स्वायम्भुवेऽन्तरे ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha caturtho śrīrājovāca devāsuranṛṇāṃ sargo nāgānāṃ mṛgapakṣiṇām sāmāsikastvayā prokto yastu svāyambhuve

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) said:
You have briefly described the genesis of gods, Asuras (demons), men, serpents, beasts and birds during the (first) Manvantara period presided over by Svāyambhuva Manu.

devanagari: तस्यैव व्यासमिच्छामि ज्ञातुं ते भगवन्यथा । अनुसर्गं यया शक्त्या ससर्ज भगवान्परः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaiva vyāsamicchāmi jñātuṃ te bhagavanyathā anusargaṃ yayā śaktyā sasarja bhagavānparaḥ

translation: I desire to know from you the details of that creation (as to) how and with what power the transcendental, glorious Lord brought for the subsequent creation, Oh venerable sage.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । इति सम्प्रश्नमाकर्ण्य राजर्षेर्बादरायणिः । प्रतिनन्द्य महायोगी जगाद मुनिसत्तमाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvāca iti sampraśnamākarṇya rājarṣerbādarāyaṇiḥ pratinandya mahāyogī jagāda munisattamāḥ

translation:
Sūta said:
On hearing this excellent query from the royal sage (Parīkṣit), the great Yogī Śuka, the son of Bāḍarāyaṇa, expressed his appreciation and spoke unto him, the noblest of sages.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । यदा प्रचेतसः पुत्रा दश प्राचीनबर्हिषः । अन्तःसमुद्रादुन्मग्ना ददृशुर्गां द्रुमैर्वृताम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca yadā pracetasaḥ putrā daśa prācīnabarhiṣaḥ antaḥsamudrādunmagnā dadṛśurgāṃ drumairvṛtām

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When the ten Pracetasas, sons of king Prācīnabarhis emerged from within the sea (-like lake) they saw that the earth was covered with trees.

devanagari: द्रुमेभ्यः क्रुध्यमानास्ते तपोदीपितमन्यवः । मुखतो वायुमग्निं च ससृजुस्तद्दिधक्षया ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: drumebhyaḥ krudhyamānāste tapodīpitamanyavaḥ mukhato vāyumagniṃ ca sasṛjustaddidhakṣayā

translation: Being enraged at the trees and with their wrath flared up by austere penance, they blew out fire and wind through their mouth, with the intention of burning down the trees.[1]

devanagari: ताभ्यां निर्दह्यमानांस्तानुपलभ्य कुरूद्वह । राजोवाच महान्सोमो मन्युं प्रशमयन्निव ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tābhyāṃ nirdahyamānāṃstānupalabhya kurūdvaha rājovāca mahānsomo manyuṃ praśamayanniva

translation: Finding the trees being reduced to ashes by them both (the fire and the wind), the great Moon-god, the ruler of the vegetable kingdom, addressed them with a view to appeasing their anger.

devanagari: न द्रुमेभ्यो महाभागा दीनेभ्यो द्रोग्धुमर्हथ । विवर्धयिषवो यूयं प्रजानां पतयः स्मृताः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: na drumebhyo mahābhāgā dīnebhyo drogdhumarhatha vivardhayiṣavo yūyaṃ prajānāṃ patayaḥ smṛtāḥ

translation: “Oh highly fortunate Princes! It does not behove you to bear enmity to the poor trees. You are regarded as the protectors of the beings the special promotion of whose growth should be your concern and desire.

devanagari: अहो प्रजापतिपतिर्भगवान्हरिरव्ययः । वनस्पतीनोषधीश्च ससर्जोर्जमिषं विभुः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: aho prajāpatipatirbhagavānhariravyayaḥ vanaspatīnoṣadhīśca sasarjorjamiṣaṃ vibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh Princes! The immutable glorious Lord Hari, the ruler of progenitors of all creatures has created the trees and the annual plants (cereals) for fruits and food-grains (or the food of the manes and food for gods).

devanagari: अन्नं चराणामचरा ह्यपदः पादचारिणाम् । अहस्ता हस्तयुक्तानां द्विपदां च चतुष्पदः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: annaṃ carāṇāmacarā hyapadaḥ pādacāriṇām ahastā hastayuktānāṃ dvipadāṃ ca catuṣpadaḥ
translation: The immobile ones (trees, plants etc. i.e. their produce like fruits and flowers) are food for the mobile creatures; the footless (i.e. grass etc.) the food of those having feet; the handless ones (e.g. the fish) are the food of those endowed with hands; and the quadrupeds (e.g. deer, boar etc.) are the food of the bipeds (human beings).

devanagari: यूयं च पित्रान्वादिष्टा देवदेवेन चानघाः । प्रजासर्गाय हि कथं वृक्षान्निर्दग्धुमर्हथ ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yūyaṃ ca pitrānvādiṣṭā devadevena cānaghāḥ prajāsargāya hi kathaṃ vṛkṣānnirdagdhumarhatha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: Oh sinless ones! Inasmuch as you are commanded by your father (Prācīnabarhis) and by the god of gods to procreate beings how is it proper for you to burn down trees (which form the food of creatures).

devanagari: आतिष्ठत सतां मार्गं कोपं यच्छत दीपितम् । पित्रा पितामहेनापि जुष्टं वः प्रपितामहैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ātiṣṭhata satāṃ mārgaṃ kopaṃ yacchata dīpitam pitrā pitāmahenāpi juṣṭaṃ vaḥ prapitāmahaiḥ

[accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Please follow the path of the righteous which was adopted by your father, grandfather and great-grandfather and control your intensified wrath.

devanagari: तोकानां पितरौ बन्धू दृशः पक्ष्म स्त्रियाः पतिः । पतिः प्रजानां भिक्षूणां गृह्यज्ञानां बुधः सुहृत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tokānāṃ pitarau bandhū dṛśaḥ pakṣma striyāḥ patiḥ patiḥ prajānāṃ bhikṣūṇāṃ gṛhyajñānāṃ budhaḥ suhṛt
translation: The parents are real friends (guardians of interests) of children; the eyelashes are of the eyes; the husband is of a woman; the king is of the subjects; the householder is the true friend of the mendicants and the teacher or the wise person is that of the ignorant. (The protectors of beings are hence the true friends of the creatures; you should not therefore, destroy the food or the means of livelihood of your subjects).

devanagari: अन्तर्देहेषु भूतानामात्मास्ते हरिरीश्वरः । सर्वं तद्धिष्ण्यमीक्षध्वमेवं वस्तोषितो ह्यसौ ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: antardeheṣu bhūtānāmātmāste harirīśvaraḥ sarvaṃ taddhiṣṇyamīkṣadhvamevaṃ vastoṣito hyasau
translation: The Soul that dwells within the bodies of the creatures is Lord Hari, the Supreme Controller; please note (carefully) that the entire creation is his abode. Hence, it is thus that he will be propitiated by you.

devanagari: यः समुत्पतितं देह आकाशान्मन्युमुल्बणम् । आत्मजिज्ञासया यच्छेत्स गुणानतिवर्तते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ samutpatitaṃ deha ākāśānmanyumulbaṇam ātmajijñāsayā yacchetsa guṇānativartate
translation: He, who, with a desire to know the real nature of the Soul, controls (within himself) sudden explosion of wrath within the cavity of his heart, goes beyond the guṇas which are the cause of the miseries of Saṃsāra).

devanagari: अलं दग्धैर्द्रुमैर्दीनैः खिलानां शिवमस्तु वः । वार्क्षी ह्येषा वरा कन्या पत्नीत्वे प्रतिगृह्यताम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: alaṃ dagdhairdrumairdīnaiḥ khilānāṃ śivamastu vaḥ vārkṣī hyeṣā varā kanyā patnītve pratigṛhyatām
translation: Enough of the helpless trees that are (so far) consumed with fire. (Spare the remaining ones). May the surviving trees and yourselves live in bliss. Here is this excellent girl (Māriṣā by name) brought up by the trees. May she be accepted by you as your wife.”

devanagari: इत्यामन्त्र्य वरारोहां कन्यामाप्सरसीं नृप । सोमो राजा ययौ दत्त्वा ते धर्मेणोपयेमिरे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ityāmantrya varārohāṃ kanyāmāpsarasīṃ nṛpa somo rājā yayau dattvā te dharmeṇopayemire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having pacified them (the Pracetasas) thus and having offered that beautiful daughter (with fine hips) of the Apsaras (Pramlocā) to them in marriage, the king Soma (the Moon-god) returned. They then married her according to religious rites.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Vide supra 4.30.13-16. As Soma brought her up by nectar from his index finger, he had the fatherly right to offer her in marriage. As Lord Viṣṇu commanded them all to marry her, religious sanction was automatically received for this polyandric marriage.

devanagari: तेभ्यस्तस्यां समभवद्दक्षः प्राचेतसः किल । यस्य प्रजाविसर्गेण लोका आपूरितास्त्रयः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyastasyāṃ samabhavaddakṣaḥ prācetasaḥ kila yasya prajāvisargeṇa lokā āpūritāstrayaḥ
translation: Dakṣa, the son of Pracetasas, they say, was born of her from them. The three worlds are fully populated by his progeny (and their descendants).

devanagari: यथा ससर्ज भूतानि दक्षो दुहितृवत्सलः । रेतसा मनसा चैव तन्ममावहितः शृणु ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā sasarja bhūtāni dakṣo duhitṛvatsalaḥ retasā manasā caiva tanmamāvahitaḥ śṛṇu

translation: Now listen to me attentively how Dakṣa who was so affectionate to his daughters procreated beings by his mind (mental faculty) as well as with his semen (physical body).

devanagari: मनसैवासृजत्पूर्वं प्रजापतिरिमाः प्रजाः । देवासुरमनुष्यादीन्नभःस्थलजलौकसः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: manasaivāsṛjatpūrvaṃ prajāpatirimāḥ prajāḥ devāsuramanuṣyādīnnabhaḥsthalajalaukasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: In the beginning, it was by his mental faculties that he procreated these beings, viz., gods, demons (Asuras), men and such other creatures dwelling in the sky, on the earth and in the water.

devanagari: तमबृंहितमालोक्य प्रजासर्गं प्रजापतिः । विन्ध्यपादानुपव्रज्य सोऽचरद्दुष्करं तपः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tamabṛṃhitamālokya prajāsargaṃ prajāpatiḥ vindhyapādānupavrajya so

translation: Finding that this creation of beings was not growing prolific, the progenitor (of creation) approached the hills near the foot of the Vindhya mountain and performed very austere penance.

devanagari: तत्राघमर्षणं नाम तीर्थं पापहरं परम् । उपस्पृश्यानुसवनं तपसातोषयद्धरिम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāghamarṣaṇaṃ nāma tīrthaṃ pāpaharaṃ param upaspṛśyānusavanaṃ tapasātoṣayaddharim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: There was an extremely holy lake efficacious to destroy sins. It was called Aghamaṛṣaṇa (efficacious to wash off sins). He bathed three times a day (in the morning, midday and evening) in the lake and propitiated Lord Hari with his austere penance.

devanagari: अस्तौषीद्धंसगुह्येन भगवन्तमधोक्षजम् । तुभ्यं तदभिधास्यामि कस्यातुष्यद्यथा हरिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: astauṣīddhaṃsaguhyena bhagavantamadhokṣajam tubhyaṃ tadabhidhāsyāmi kasyātuṣyadyathā hariḥ
translation: He praised the glorious Lord Viṣṇu with the Haṃsaguhya hymn. I shall now recite that hymn with which Hari was pleased with Dakṣa.

devanagari: श्रीप्रजापतिरुवाच । नमः परायावितथानुभूतये गुणत्रयाभासनिमित्तबन्धवे । अदृष्टधाम्ने गुणतत्त्वबुद्धिभिर्निवृत्तमानाय दधे स्वयम्भुवे ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprajāpatiruvāca namaḥ parāyāvitathānubhūtaye guṇatrayābhāsanimittabandhave adṛṣṭadhāmne guṇatattvabuddhibhirnivṛttamānāya dadhe svayambhuve

translation:
Prajāpati (Dakṣa) said:
I pay obeisance to the transcendental Lord whose power of consciousness is not unreal; who is the controller of jīva (the individual Soul as reflected through the three guṇas of Māyā) and the Māyā (the cause or the basis of such reflection); whose form or true nature is invisible to those who look upon guṇas or objects of senses as real; who is beyond all the means of knowledge (e.g. inference etc.) who is beyond limit of time and space, and Self-effulgent.

devanagari: न यस्य सख्यं पुरुषोऽवैति सख्युः सखा वसन्संवसतः पुरेऽस्मिन् । गुणो यथा गुणिनो व्यक्तदृष्टेस्तस्मै महेशाय नमस्करोमि ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: na yasya sakhyaṃ puruṣo guṇo yathā guṇino vyaktadṛṣṭestasmai maheśāya namaskaromi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: I bow to the Supreme Lord who is the friend of the jīva inhabiting the same city (body) along with him but of whose friendliness (in activating the sense-organs and the mind) the jīva is not aware. He is the seer of the world (and hence unseen by the world) just as a guṇa (the object of cognition) cannot perceive the possessor of the guṇa (viz. the cognitive ability of the cognizing sense).

devanagari: देहोऽसवोऽक्षा मनवो भूतमात्रामात्मानमन्यं च विदुः परं यत् । सर्वं पुमान्वेद गुणांश्च तज्ज्ञो न वेद सर्वज्ञमनन्तमीडे ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: deho sarvaṃ pumānveda guṇāṃśca tajjño na veda sarvajñamanantamīḍe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The body, the vital breaths, the senses, the internal organs or mental faculties, the five bhūtas (gross elements) and their tanmātrās (subtle forms e.g. sound, touch etc.) do not know either themselves or the others and their presiding deities who are beyond them all (as all these have no consciousness). The jīva (being consciousness by nature) knows all the guṇas (the root cause of the above-mentioned list). But the jīva who knows them (the above) does not know the Omniscient Lord who is Infinite. Him I praise.

devanagari: यदोपरामो मनसो नामरूप रूपस्य दृष्टस्मृतिसम्प्रमोषात् । य ईयते केवलया स्वसंस्थया हंसाय तस्मै शुचिसद्मने नमः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yadoparāmo manaso nāmarūpa rūpasya dṛṣṭasmṛtisampramoṣāt ya īyate kevalayā svasaṃsthayā haṃsāya tasmai śucisadmane namaḥ
translation: When the mind which cognizes the world of names and forms ceases to function due to the loss or obsolescence of all cognition and memory, in the state of samādhi or complete absorption in the self, he (the Supreme Brahman) reveals himself through his inherent existence, consciousness and bliss. I offer my salutations to him whose abode is the pure heart.

devanagari: मनीषिणोऽन्तर्हृदि सन्निवेशितं स्वशक्तिभिर्नवभिश्च त्रिवृद्भिः । वह्निं यथा दारुणि पाञ्चदश्यं मनीषया निष्कर्षन्ति गूढम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: manīṣiṇo vahniṃ yathā dāruṇi pāñcadaśyaṃ manīṣayā niṣkarṣanti gūḍham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ guh class 1 verb]

translation: With their purified intellect, the self-controlled, discerning persons discover (and meditate upon) him (as distinct from ahaṃkāra) as enthroned in the innermost heart, even though he lies unmanifest by his nine powers[3] (viz. Prakṛti, mahat or cosmic intelligence, ahaṃkāra or ego, mind and tanmātras (five subtle bhūtas) and (by mind, five gross elements and ten sense-organs) and influenced by three guṇas (viz. Sattva, rajas, and tamas). (The above-mentioned wise persons do so) just as the experts in sacrificial lore separate (by friction) the sacrificial fire latent in the (piece of) wood by reciting the fifteen Sāmidhenī verses[4] (in the course of churning out fire).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī mentions a different list of nine potencies, viz., Vimalā, Utkarṣaṇī, Jñānakriyā etc. and the three powers (trivṛdbhiḥ) are Will, Knowledge and Action (Kriyā).

Footnote [4]:
Viṣṇu is called the fifteenth Principle (Pāñcadaśya). The remaining fourteen are: Ten sense-organs (indriyas), mind, intelligence (buddhi), vital airs (Prāṇa) and Pradhāna. Viṣṇu controls these—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: स वै ममाशेषविशेषमाया निषेधनिर्वाणसुखानुभूतिः । स सर्वनामा स च विश्वरूपः प्रसीदतामनिरुक्तात्मशक्तिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai mamāśeṣaviśeṣamāyā niṣedhanirvāṇasukhānubhūtiḥ sa sarvanāmā sa ca viśvarūpaḥ prasīdatāmaniruktātmaśaktiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He is realized as the highest bliss in meditation by the negation of Māyā which creates all distinctions and differences. Verily he bears every name, and assumes all forms. He possesses within him innumerable potencies which are beyond the power of words to describe. (May the Lord be gracious unto me).

devanagari: यद्यन्निरुक्तं वचसा निरूपितं धियाक्षभिर्वा मनसा वोत यस्य । मा भूत्स्वरूपं गुणरूपं हि तत्तत्स वै गुणापायविसर्गलक्षणः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyanniruktaṃ vacasā nirūpitaṃ dhiyākṣabhirvā manasā vota yasya mā bhūtsvarūpaṃ guṇarūpaṃ hi tattatsa vai guṇāpāyavisargalakṣaṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Whatever is described in words, concluded (to be true) by the intellect, perceived by the senses or imagined by the mind, is only a phenomenon of the (three) guṇas and cannot be his essential nature; for he is verily characterised by the creation or dissolution of guṇas (i.e. their product, the universe).

devanagari: यस्मिन्यतो येन च यस्य यस्मै यद्यो यथा कुरुते कार्यते च । परावरेषां परमं प्राक्प्रसिद्धं तद्ब्रह्म तद्धेतुरनन्यदेकम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yasminyato yena ca yasya yasmai yadyo yathā kurute kāryate ca parāvareṣāṃ paramaṃ prākprasiddhaṃ tadbrahma taddheturananyadekam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: It is Brahman on which everything depends; from it everything is caused; it has created everything from itself with itself. Everything is for it and belongs to it. Whatever it makes and causes others to make is Brahman. It is the ultimate cause of all causes both earlier (e.g. god Brahmā) or later. As the primary cause, it is known to have preceded all causes. It is one without a second, whether of its own kind or of another category. It is Brahman to whom I bow[5].
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā takes the Viśiṣṭādvaita position in which Īśvara is the Soul and jīva and jagat (unintelligent matter) form his body. The different cases in this verse show the intimate relation of Īśvara with others.

devanagari: यच्छक्तयो वदतां वादिनां वै विवादसंवादभुवो भवन्ति । कुर्वन्ति चैषां मुहुरात्ममोहं तस्मै नमोऽनन्तगुणाय भूम्ने ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yacchaktayo vadatāṃ vādināṃ vai vivādasaṃvādabhuvo bhavanti kurvanti caiṣāṃ muhurātmamohaṃ tasmai namo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: (If Brahman is the cause of the universe, why do the different schools of thoughts like the followers of Mīmāṃsā, Nyāya, indulge in disputation?). It is his potencies (such as Māyā) which have verily become the grounds (causes) for disputations and concurrence for various eloquent theoreticians (of different schools of thought) and which (in spite of the exhortations of the knowers of the truth to these disputants) delude their minds now and again. I bow to him, the omnipresent Lord of infinite excellences.

devanagari: अस्तीति नास्तीति च वस्तुनिष्ठयोरेकस्थयोर्भिन्नविरुद्धधर्मणोः । अवेक्षितं किञ्चन योगसाङ्ख्ययोः समं परं ह्यनुकूलं बृहत्तत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: astīti nāstīti ca vastuniṣṭhayorekasthayorbhinnaviruddhadharmaṇoḥ avekṣitaṃ kiñcana yogasāṅkhyayoḥ samaṃ paraṃ hyanukūlaṃ bṛhattat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: (Yoga, as a science of divine worship, conceives of God as a cosmic person with Pātāla, subterranean regions, as his feet, while Sankhya which is the Science of Knowledge or self-realization regards him as formless without any hands or feet.) The Yoga and Sāṅkhya systems of philosophy which proclaim faith in one and the same Reality predicate the existence and nonexistence of (two) distinct and mutually contradictory attributes to be subsisting in the same Reality (viz. possession and non-possession of feet etc.). But the common basis on which they agree and (is thus beyond dispute) is the existence of God—a Reality beyond dispute. It is the great thing—Brahman (to whom I bow).

devanagari: योऽनुग्रहार्थं भजतां पादमूलमनामरूपो भगवाननन्तः । नामानि रूपाणि च जन्मकर्मभिर्भेजे स मह्यं परमः प्रसीदतु ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yo nāmāni rūpāṇi ca janmakarmabhirbheje sa mahyaṃ paramaḥ prasīdatu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: May that glorious Supreme Lord of infinite attributes be gracious unto me—the Lord who though above material names and forms, invested himself with (absolutely pure sāttvic) forms by incarnating himself, and assumed names by his (wonderful) deeds, for the sake of bestowing his grace on those who seek shelter at his feet.

devanagari: यः प्राकृतैर्ज्ञानपथैर्जनानां यथाशयं देहगतो विभाति । यथानिलः पार्थिवमाश्रितो गुणं स ईश्वरो मे कुरुतां मनोरथम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ prākṛtairjñānapathairjanānāṃ yathāśayaṃ dehagato vibhāti yathānilaḥ pārthivamāśrito guṇaṃ sa īśvaro me kurutāṃ manoratham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: May that Supreme Ruler grant me my desired object(s)—the Supreme Lord who abiding in all bodies as the (formless) Inner controller, manifests himself in the forms of different deities to people according to the tendencies (formed by impressions of the actions of their previous births) and their paths of worship of recent origin, just as a breeze of wind (though itself devoid of the characteristics of the earth, viz. fragrance or colour) bears different kinds of smells according to the fragrance of the flowers with which it comes into contact or looks whitish, the attribute of dust mixed with it.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति स्तुतः संस्तुवतः स तस्मिन्नघमर्षणे । प्रादुरासीत्कुरुश्रेष्ठ भगवान्भक्तवत्सलः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti stutaḥ saṃstuvataḥ sa tasminnaghamarṣaṇe prādurāsītkuruśreṣṭha bhagavānbhaktavatsalaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus praised (with the hymns), the glorious Lord who is affectionate to his devotees manifested himself to Dakṣa while he was extolling him in the sacred lake called Aghamarṣaṇa, oh foremost Kuru.

devanagari: कृतपादः सुपर्णांसे प्रलम्बाष्टमहाभुजः । चक्रशङ्खासिचर्मेषु धनुःपाशगदाधरः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtapādaḥ suparṇāṃse pralambāṣṭamahābhujaḥ cakraśaṅkhāsicarmeṣu dhanuḥpāśagadādharaḥ

translation: Riding with his feet flung on both the shoulders of Garuḍa, he wielded in his extraordinarily long and mighty arms a discus, a conch, a sword,a shield, an arrow, a noose and a mace (in each respectively).

devanagari: पीतवासा घनश्यामः प्रसन्नवदनेक्षणः । वनमालानिवीताङ्गो लसच्छ्रीवत्सकौस्तुभः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: pītavāsā ghanaśyāmaḥ prasannavadanekṣaṇaḥ vanamālānivītāṅgo lasacchrīvatsakaustubhaḥ

translation: He was clad in yellow silken garment with complexion dark like a cloud, his countenance and eyes were beaming with pleasure and joy. His person was adorned with Vanamālā (a garland of forest-flowers) and bore the mark called Śrīvatsa and the valuable gem Kaustubha.

devanagari: महाकिरीटकटकः स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलः । काञ्च्यङ्गुलीयवलय नूपुराङ्गदभूषितः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mahākirīṭakaṭakaḥ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalaḥ kāñcyaṅgulīyavalaya nūpurāṅgadabhūṣitaḥ

[nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]

translation: He wore a very costly crown and bracelets and radiant pair of crocodile-shaped ear-rings. He was adorned with zones, rings, bracelets, anklets and armlets.

devanagari: त्रैलोक्यमोहनं रूपं बिभ्रत्त्रिभुवनेश्वरः । वृतो नारदनन्दाद्यैः पार्षदैः सुरयूथपैः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: trailokyamohanaṃ rūpaṃ bibhrattribhuvaneśvaraḥ vṛto nāradanandādyaiḥ pārṣadaiḥ surayūthapaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: The Lord of the three worlds manifested a beautiful form captivating the three worlds. He was surrounded by Nārada, Nanda and other attendants and by protectors of the worlds (like Indra, Varuṇa). He was eulogized by Siddhas, Gandharvas and Cāraṇas (the celestial singers and others) who followed him singing.

devanagari: स्तूयमानोऽनुगायद्भिः सिद्धगन्धर्वचारणैः । रूपं तन्महदाश्चर्यं विचक्ष्यागतसाध्वसः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: stūyamāno rūpaṃ tanmahadāścaryaṃ vicakṣyāgatasādhvasaḥ
translation: On seeing that most wonderful form (of the Lord), the progenitor Dakṣa was overcome with awe, but his heart was overjoyed. He lay prostrate before him on the ground (like a rod) and bowed to him.

devanagari: ननाम दण्डवद्भूमौ प्रहृष्टात्मा प्रजापतिः । न किञ्चनोदीरयितुमशकत्तीव्रया मुदा । आपूरितमनोद्वारैर्ह्रदिन्य इव निर्झरैः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: nanāma daṇḍavadbhūmau prahṛṣṭātmā prajāpatiḥ na kiñcanodīrayitumaśakattīvrayā mudā āpūritamanodvārairhradinya iva nirjharaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Just as rivers are overflooded by streams, his senses were too full with intense joy to be able to speak. Thus, on account of excessive joy he could not utter a word.

devanagari: तं तथावनतं भक्तं प्रजाकामं प्रजापतिम् । चित्तज्ञः सर्वभूतानामिदमाह जनार्दनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tathāvanataṃ bhaktaṃ prajākāmaṃ prajāpatim cittajñaḥ sarvabhūtānāmidamāha janārdanaḥ
translation: Lord Viṣṇu who knows the minds of all created beings spoke thus to the progenitor devotee Dakṣa who had laid himself prostrate that way before him and was desirous of procreating progeny.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । प्राचेतस महाभाग संसिद्धस्तपसा भवान् । यच्छ्रद्धया मत्परया मयि भावं परं गतः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca prācetasa mahābhāga saṃsiddhastapasā bhavān yacchraddhayā matparayā mayi bhāvaṃ paraṃ gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Venerable Lord said:
Oh highly fortunate son of Pracetas! You have achieved the objective of your austere penance inasmuch as you have cherished supreme devotion unto me through your unswerving intense faith in me.

devanagari: प्रीतोऽहं ते प्रजानाथ यत्तेऽस्योद्बृंहणं तपः । ममैष कामो भूतानां यद्भूयासुर्विभूतयः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: prīto mamaiṣa kāmo bhūtānāṃ yadbhūyāsurvibhūtayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yat class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yat class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yat class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh Lord of created beings! I am pleased with you as the growth of the universe is the aim of your penance. It is also my desire that there should be all round growth and prosperity of all created beings.

devanagari: ब्रह्मा भवो भवन्तश्च मनवो विबुधेश्वराः । विभूतयो मम ह्येता भूतानां भूतिहेतवः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmā bhavo bhavantaśca manavo vibudheśvarāḥ vibhūtayo mama hyetā bhūtānāṃ bhūtihetavaḥ
translation: God Brahmā, Śiva, you all Prajāpatis (Lords of created beings), Manus (progenitors of human beings who preside over certain epochs or Manvantaras) and the foremost deities—all are my special incarnations for the prosperity and procreation of created beings.

devanagari: तपो मे हृदयं ब्रह्मंस्तनुर्विद्या क्रियाकृतिः । अङ्गानि क्रतवो जाता धर्म आत्मासवः सुराः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tapo me hṛdayaṃ brahmaṃstanurvidyā kriyākṛtiḥ aṅgāni kratavo jātā dharma ātmāsavaḥ surāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: [6] Oh Brahman (Dakṣa)! Contemplation (accompanied with disciplines of Yama and Niyama) is my heart. The spiritual lore (the internal repetition of Mantras with the specific technique of nyāsa etc. which results in concrete meditation and my realization) is my body. The activities pertaining to contemplation is my form. Accurately performed sacrifices are the limbs of my body. The merit (accruing from the well-performed sacrifices) is my Soul. Gods (the recipients of oblations in the sacrifices) form my very life.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā, Siddhāntapradīpa explain that Lord wants to emphasize the importance of contemplation, sacrifices etc. as they are dear to him.
Bālaprabodhini explains that the Lord reminds Dakṣa of his previous birth and proficiency in sacrifice by calling him ‘Brahman’.

devanagari: अहमेवासमेवाग्रे नान्यत्किञ्चान्तरं बहिः । संज्ञानमात्रमव्यक्तं प्रसुप्तमिव विश्वतः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: ahamevāsamevāgre nānyatkiñcāntaraṃ bahiḥ saṃjñānamātramavyaktaṃ prasuptamiva viśvataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: At the beginning of creation, I alone existed (without any activity). There was nothing else as internal (the seer or knower) or external (the seen or the object of knowledge). I was pure consciousness and unmanifested. There was (as if) deep sleep (no activity) everywhere.

devanagari: मय्यनन्तगुणेऽनन्ते गुणतो गुणविग्रहः । यदासीत्तत एवाद्यः स्वयम्भूः समभूदजः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: mayyanantaguṇe yadāsīttata evādyaḥ svayambhūḥ samabhūdajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: In me who am Infinite and endowed with an infinite number of attributes, there evolved through Māyā (constituted of three guṇas) this universe (a product of guṇas). At that time the selfborn Brahmā, the First Person (the creator of all) arose (along with the universe).

devanagari: स वै यदा महादेवो मम वीर्योपबृंहितः । मेने खिलमिवात्मानमुद्यतः स्वर्गकर्मणि ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai yadā mahādevo mama vīryopabṛṃhitaḥ mene khilamivātmānamudyataḥ svargakarmaṇi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: When the great god Brahmā, though supplied with my energy, became engaged in the work of creation, he felt that he was not sufficiently competent for it.

devanagari: अथ मेऽभिहितो देवस्तपोऽतप्यत दारुणम् । नव विश्वसृजो युष्मान्येनादावसृजद्विभुः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: atha me nava viśvasṛjo yuṣmānyenādāvasṛjadvibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]
[imperfect active first dual], [aorist active first dual]

translation: Then as per my command, the god Brahmā performed very austere penance. Empowered by it, he created nine progenitors of created beings including you (in your previous birth) in the beginning.

devanagari: एषा पञ्चजनस्याङ्ग दुहिता वै प्रजापतेः । असिक्नी नाम पत्नीत्वे प्रजेश प्रतिगृह्यताम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣā pañcajanasyāṅga duhitā vai prajāpateḥ asiknī nāma patnītve prajeśa pratigṛhyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Oh dear child! Here is the daughter of the progenitor of creatures, Pañcajana. Her name is Asiknī. Accept her as your wife, Oh Lord of created beings.

devanagari: मिथुनव्यवायधर्मस्त्वं प्रजासर्गमिमं पुनः । मिथुनव्यवायधर्मिण्यां भूरिशो भावयिष्यसि ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: mithunavyavāyadharmastvaṃ prajāsargamimaṃ punaḥ mithunavyavāyadharmiṇyāṃ bhūriśo bhāvayiṣyasi
translation: You have to follow the righteous course of sexual union between a duly married couple. You will procreate progeny in large numbers from her who will also observe the same righteous duty (of sexual intercourse between the duly married) like you.

devanagari: त्वत्तोऽधस्तात्प्रजाः सर्वा मिथुनीभूय मायया । मदीयया भविष्यन्ति हरिष्यन्ति च मे बलिम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: tvatto madīyayā bhaviṣyanti hariṣyanti ca me balim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṝ class 9 verb]

translation: All created beings after you will be born through sexual intercourse with women, under the influence of my Māyā and they will offer worship.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्त्वा मिषतस्तस्य भगवान्विश्वभावनः । स्वप्नोपलब्धार्थ इव तत्रैवान्तर्दधे हरिः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityuktvā miṣatastasya bhagavānviśvabhāvanaḥ svapnopalabdhārtha iva tatraivāntardadhe hariḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having addressed him [thus, Lord Hari the prime cause of the universe vanished on that very spot like an object seen in a dream, while Dakṣa was looking on.
